category_title: Lower-proficiency students who use L1 and gestures to express concepts in content-subject lessons
scenario_title: Explaining plate tectonics using gestures and L1
minimal_context: The student is answering questions about how tectonic plates move during a Geography lesson.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: When plates move, why will it shake? How can plates move?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Can you try to answer the question?

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Yes, but how can plates move?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: That way, that way, that way. [hands gesturing]

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: When plates move, why will it shake? How can plates move?

STUDENT: (Remains silent)

TUTOR: Can you try to answer the question?

STUDENT: Move...

TUTOR: Yes, but how can plates move?

STUDENT: That way, that way, that way. [hands gesturing]

task.content: Answering the questions: 'When plates move, why will it shake? (pause) How can plates move?'
task.learning_goal: Explaining plate movement in a Geography lesson

context.environment: Geography lesson in School C, a Content and Language Integrated Learning (CLIL) classroom
context.social_setting: Teacher-student interaction during a content-subject lesson

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When asked an open-ended academic question in L2 ('How can plates move?')
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Used L1 (translated as 'That way, that way, that way') and physical gestures ('hands gesturing') instead of L2 academic language
related_quotes: That way, that way, that way. [hands gesturing]

behavior_patterns.condition: When participating in teacher-student interaction during observed lessons
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Provides very short verbal responses, with a mean length of student turns at 1.9 s (SD = 1.6)

behavior_patterns.condition: When teachers ask questions in L2
